title: Migrate CI to GitHub Actions and stop CircleCI checkout failures
description: Main is red because CircleCI fails before build/test in the checkout step: its SSH clone has no key. Move the repository-owned CI to GitHub Actions for build/test verification, and keep the repository config from continuing to fail in CircleCI during the transition.
acceptance_criteria: A GitHub Actions workflow in the repo runs the build and test quality gates on push and pull requests. The README CI badge points at the active workflow. Repository config no longer fails during CircleCI checkout for normal pushes, either because CircleCI is made non-failing or because the in-repo configuration is updated appropriately for the transition. Remote CI is verified after push, not just locally.

CircleCI still reports on the branch, but no longer fails at checkout: commit status context  succeeded and points to https://circleci.com/gh/jonbaldie/avg/17. Operational effect of the CircleCI config change: CircleCI remains green with a no-op transition job while repository-owned build/test enforcement moves to GitHub Actions.
Manager verified the original red main failure remotely before fixing it: CircleCI build 15 failed in Checkout code with git@github.com publickey permission denied. Implemented GitHub Actions in-repo, switched the README badge and CI wording to GitHub Actions, changed CircleCI to a temporary no-op transition job, updated test_cli.sh to avoid redundant rebuild races, and then pushed main. Verified commit 42ede72 on main after push: GitHub Actions run 25491375161 succeeded and CircleCI transition build 19 succeeded. Branch protection is not configured on main, so no required-check migration was needed.

title: Harden CLI input validation and child command failure handling
description: The avg CLI currently crashes on missing or malformed arguments and can print an average even when the measured command fails. Tighten argument parsing, reject invalid iteration counts, and make child command failures explicit so the core tool is trustworthy.
acceptance_criteria: Running with missing args or malformed iteration input exits cleanly with a helpful error. Zero and negative iteration counts are rejected. A non-zero exit from the measured command causes avg itself to fail with a clear message instead of silently printing an average. The implementation is developed with red/green TDD and no mocks, includes at least one automated regression check, and includes a manual execution note answering 'But does it actually work?'
notes:
Completion summary:
- Implemented hardened CLI parsing in `Main.hs` so missing args now emit `Usage: avg "<command>" <iterations>`, malformed iteration input reports the bad value, and zero/negative iteration counts are rejected before execution.
- Child command handling now stops on the first non-zero exit and `avg` exits non-zero with `Command failed with exit code <n>` instead of printing a misleading average.
- Red/green TDD used a real executable regression script (`test_cli.sh`) with no mocks. First red failure was the missing-args crash; green run passed after the hardening change.
- Manual execution also verified the bad-input and child-failure cases plus a successful `true` run. But does it actually work? Yes in this worktree: the CLI now fails clearly on invalid input and child errors, and succeeds cleanly on valid input.

title: Prepare worktree scaffold and delegate initial worker beads
description: Set up the repository for isolated worker worktrees, ensure .worktrees is present and ignored, and launch short-lived worker sessions on concrete beads with merge-ready instructions.
acceptance_criteria: .worktrees/ exists in the repo root and is gitignored. At least two child beads are created or updated for delegation. Worker instructions cite specific bead IDs, require bd prime on startup, require red/green TDD without mocks, require manual execution, and require detailed bead notes.
notes:
Manager coordination task. Ask repeatedly: But does it actually work?
Completed manager setup and delegation: created and gitignored .worktrees/, created focused child beads, launched isolated workers for avg-y77.2 and avg-y77.3, reviewed both branches skeptically, integrated the accepted changes with follow-up beads avg-y77.5 and avg-y77.6, and removed the worker worktrees with bd worktree remove after review.

title: Baseline reliability tranche for avg CLI
description: Create a small, well-defined tranche of work to harden the avg CLI and its delivery workflow. This epic groups short-lived tasks that can be delegated safely to isolated worker agents.
acceptance_criteria: Focused child issues exist for CLI validation, CI reliability, and any immediate follow-up discovered during execution. Coordination notes capture merge decisions and follow-up dependencies.
notes:
Manager-owned epic for parallel delegation and merge review.
Merge review outcome: avg-y77.2 was accepted functionally and then polished with avg-y77.6 to send diagnostics to stderr. avg-y77.3 provided the CI/test scaffold, but the initial happy-path-only test was strengthened during integration under avg-y77.5 so the shared make test target now runs the real CLI regression script. avg-y77.4 remains open for README alignment.
Epic complete: worker-delivered CLI hardening, CI regression coverage, stderr diagnostics, and README alignment are merged or integrated on main. Follow-up bug avg-9ow was captured during docs verification for the flaky repeated rebuild path.

title: Investigate flaky repeated ghc rebuilds in worktree
description:
Repeated local rebuilds in the same worktree sometimes fail with GHC rename errors like rename 'Main.o.tmp' to 'Main.o': does not exist. This showed up while validating the README docs because rm -rf build && mkdir -p build && ghc -O2 Main.hs -o build/avg
[2 of 2] Linking build/avg and chmod +x test_cli.sh && ./test_cli.sh
test_cli.sh: all checks passed can fail after a prior build, even though a forced serial rebuild succeeds. Investigate the root cause and make the documented build/test workflow reliably repeatable.

title: Strengthen make test to run CLI regression coverage in CI
description: The initial make test target only checks the happy path, so CI would still miss regressions in CLI validation and child-command failure handling. Update the test target to run the real CLI regression script once the hardening changes are merged.
acceptance_criteria: The repository has a single non-interactive test entry point used by CI. That test entry point covers missing args, malformed iteration input, non-positive iteration counts, child command failure, and a successful run using real command execution and no mocks. CircleCI runs that strengthened test target.
notes:
Created during manager review of avg-y77.3 before merge. This is an integration-quality follow-up, not a duplicate of the original test-target task.
Discovered during merge review: avg-y77.3 added the CI/test scaffold, but its test only covers the happy path. Manager is integrating the stronger CLI regression script into the shared make/CI entry point before accepting the tranche.
Manager integration plan: take the worker CLI regression script, make it the shared make test entry point for CI, verify the combined branch locally, and only then accept the worker changes.
Manager integrated the worker branches by making test_cli.sh the shared non-interactive test entry point, wiring make test to it, and keeping CircleCI on checkout plus build/test/clean gates. Verification: make clean && make build && make test && make clean.

title: Add non-interactive test target and run it in CircleCI
description: The project currently builds in CI but has no real test target. Add a non-interactive make-based test workflow and update CircleCI to validate the checked-out revision with explicit quality gates.
acceptance_criteria: The make test target exists and runs non-interactively. CircleCI uses checkout rather than manually cloning the repository. CI runs the project build plus the new test target. The work is developed with red/green TDD and no mocks, includes at least one manual execution note answering But does it actually work, and leaves detailed bead notes about exact commands run.
notes: Exact commands run: `make test` (red, failed: no test target); `make test` (failed after first implementation because Make escaped `$` incorrectly in the grep anchor); `make test`; `printf '2\\n4\\n6\\n' | make test`; `make build`; `make test`; `make clean`; `make clean && make build && make test && make clean && make build && printf 'echo hello\\n' | ./build/avg "sh" 1`. Manual execution result: the built binary executed `sh` with stdin `echo hello`, printed `hello`, then printed an average duration line. But does it actually work? Yes for the implemented scope: the new non-interactive make target passes locally and the manual CLI flow works. Noted caveat: running multiple `ghc` builds in parallel in the same worktree races on `Main.o`, so verification was rerun serially.
close_reason: Completed: added non-interactive make test target, updated CircleCI to checkout and run build/test quality gates, and verified the flow locally with red/green notes recorded.

title: Retire CircleCI transition shim after external project disablement
description: The repository now uses GitHub Actions for real build/test gates. CircleCI has been reduced to a no-op transition job so main stays green, but the external CircleCI project is still active and reports a redundant status. Once CircleCI is disabled outside the repo, remove the shim config cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: The external CircleCI project is disabled or disconnected. The .circleci/config.yml transition file is removed or intentionally archived without leaving red statuses. Repository docs and badges still point only at the active GitHub Actions workflow.
notes: Discovered during avg-khu. This is cleanup, not a blocker: main is green and GitHub Actions is the active CI.

title: Send CLI usage and failure diagnostics to stderr
description: The new CLI hardening path exits non-zero correctly, but diagnostic messages still go to stdout. Route usage and failure messages to stderr so the tool behaves better in scripts and CI.
acceptance_criteria: Usage errors and child-command failure messages are written to stderr while successful average output remains on stdout. Existing regression coverage continues to pass using real command execution and no mocks.
notes:
Discovered during manager review of avg-y77.2. Small CLI polish task; not a blocker for the main hardening behavior.
Manager plans a minimal follow-up during integration: route usage and failure diagnostics to stderr while keeping successful timing output on stdout and preserving the new regression coverage.
Manager changed CLI diagnostics to stderr via hPutStrLn stderr while keeping average output on stdout. Verification included redirect checks confirming child-command failures produce stderr output with no stdout leakage.

title: Fix make build target freshness
description: The current Makefile uses a directory named  as the target, so make: `build' is up to date. can incorrectly report the target up to date after source changes. This forces tests to use  and risks stale binaries during manual verification. Update the build target structure so ordinary make: `build' is up to date. reliably rebuilds when inputs change.
notes: Discovered during avg-y77.2 red/green TDD. `make build` can skip recompilation after `Main.hs` changes because the target name collides with the output directory. Current workaround in regression tests is `make build --always-make`, but the underlying Makefile should be corrected in follow-up work.
close_reason: Resolved during manager integration by declaring the Makefile build target phony and cleaning build artifacts, so ordinary make build no longer relies on directory freshness.

title: Refresh README to match validated CLI, test, and install behavior
description: Update the README only after the CLI behavior and test workflow are concrete, so docs reflect reality instead of aspirations.
acceptance_criteria: README documents validated CLI usage, build/install steps, and quoting expectations. Documentation matches the implemented failure behavior and test workflow. Any stale roadmap items related to tests or installation are updated accurately.
notes:
Kickoff plan: run the current README commands and inspect the validated CLI/test/build workflow first, note every mismatch, then update README to match reality, rerun the documented commands manually, and leave final notes answering 'But does it actually work?' with exact commands and outcomes.
Manager review found one docs regression before merge: the README changed the Docker example to omit the explicit avg command, but the image uses CMD rather than ENTRYPOINT. Verified with docker run: Average elapsed time: 0.023654173s prints an average, while  does not run avg. Fixing the README before accepting the docs branch.
Manager accepted the README refresh after one integration fix: the Docker example must invoke avg explicitly because the published image uses CMD rather than ENTRYPOINT. Verified locally with docker run and the documented local build/test commands.
